title: Audit derived docs
goal: Execute the doc-audit maintenance pass in one run. Inspect the derived docs, compare them to the expected schema and source-agent runtime state, then write the audit report and audit runtime state. Do not stop at analysis: the task is only complete after the report and state file are written.
extra_context:
Allowed scope only: demo/automation_poller/daily_notes_doc/fixtures/workspace/memory/daily-notes, demo/automation_poller/daily_notes_doc/fixtures/workspace/memory/doc/daily-note-derived, demo/automation_poller/daily_notes_doc/jobs/daily-notes-to-doc/state, and demo/automation_poller/daily_notes_doc/jobs/doc-audit.

Perform these steps in order:
1. Read demo/automation_poller/daily_notes_doc/jobs/daily-notes-to-doc/state/runtime-state.json.
2. Read the required derived docs under demo/automation_poller/daily_notes_doc/fixtures/workspace/memory/doc/daily-note-derived: README.md, current-state.md, decisions.md, open-questions.md, source-map.md.
3. List all *.md files in demo/automation_poller/daily_notes_doc/fixtures/workspace/memory/daily-notes and compare them with processed_notes from the source-agent state.

Audit rules:
- Fail if any required derived doc file is missing.
- Fail if current-state.md is missing any of: ## Stable Signals, ## Active Workstreams, ## Recent Changes, ## Coverage.
- Fail if decisions.md is missing any of: ## Active Decisions, ## Archived Decisions.
- Fail if open-questions.md is missing any of: ## Open Questions, ## Recently Resolved.
- Fail if source-map.md is missing any of: ## Processed Notes, ## Coverage Summary.
- Fail if current-state.md, decisions.md, or open-questions.md contain substantive content without any Source notes: citations.
- Fail if any cited daily-note path does not exist.
- Fail if source-map.md contains duplicate processed-note rows.
- Fail if source-map.md note order does not match source-agent processed_notes.
- Fail if newer unprocessed daily notes exist.
- Pass only if all checks succeed.

Then write demo/automation_poller/daily_notes_doc/jobs/doc-audit/state/runtime-state.json as JSON with at least these keys:
- run_count
- last_run_started_at
- last_run_finished_at
- next_run_at
- last_status
- last_report_path
- last_findings
State rules:
- Increment run_count by 1 from the previous state.
- Set last_run_started_at and last_run_finished_at to this run's timestamps in ISO-8601 format.
- Set next_run_at to roughly 20 seconds after last_run_started_at, because this demo config polls every 20 seconds.
- Set last_status to pass or fail.

Then write one markdown report under demo/automation_poller/daily_notes_doc/jobs/doc-audit/reports named <timestamp>-doc-audit.md. The report must include:
- heading '# Doc Audit Report'
- run time
- status line
- section '## Findings'
If the audit passes, include one finding line that clearly says there were no findings. If the audit fails, list concrete reasons.

Do not repair derived docs in this task. Use apply_patch for edits. Do not inspect unrelated directories. Finish with one short sentence that states pass or fail.
